display.purpose: Page titles and club identity headers only — uppercase, condensed.

body.purpose: The default UI font for nearly everything: tables, buttons, forms, copy.

label.purpose: Table headers, stat-card labels, badges — smallest, densest text.

shadows.name: elevation-1
shadows.purpose: Barely-there lift for a surface just above its background.

shadows.name: elevation-2
shadows.purpose: Floating overlays — sort menus, dropdowns, popovers.

shadows.name: elevation-3
shadows.purpose: Modals and the most detached surfaces.

motion.name: ease-normal
motion.purpose: Default transition for most state changes.

components.name: Primary Button (Continuar)
components.description: The week-advance action button, repeated in every PageHeader.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary">Continuar <svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M5 12h14M12 5l7 7-7 7"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:8px; padding:9px 18px; border-radius:8px; border:none; background:#3d7bf5; color:#fff; font-family:'Inter',sans-serif; font-weight:600; font-size:13px; cursor:pointer; transition:filter 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter:brightness(1.1); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #3d7bf5; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity:0.6; cursor:default; }

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Circular quick-nav button used in the topbar (Visão do Clube, Classificação, etc).
components.html: <button class="ds-icon-btn" title="Classificação"><svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"/><path d="M12 6v6l4 2"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-icon-btn { width:30px; height:30px; border-radius:50%; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; background:transparent; border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); color:#9aa0ad; cursor:pointer; transition:all 150ms ease-out; } .ds-icon-btn:hover { color:#e8eaf0; border-color:rgba(255,255,255,0.12); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Tinted-background / solid-text badge used for zones, grades, and status labels.
components.css: .ds-badge { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:4px; padding:3px 8px; border-radius:6px; font-size:10px; font-weight:600; background:#20242e; color:#9aa0ad; } .ds-badge--green { background:rgba(63,191,107,0.15); color:#3fbf6b; } .ds-badge--amber { background:rgba(224,179,65,0.15); color:#e0b341; } .ds-badge--red { background:rgba(226,92,82,0.15); color:#e25c52; }

components.name: Stat Bar
components.description: Color-coded progress bar for fitness/form/morale, threshold colors from utils/statusColors.ts.
components.css: .ds-statbar__label { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; margin-bottom:4px; font-size:10px; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.04em; color:#6b7080; } .ds-statbar__track { height:6px; background:#20242e; border-radius:3px; overflow:hidden; } .ds-statbar__fill { height:100%; border-radius:3px; background:#3fbf6b; }

components.name: Club Crest
components.description: Procedural shield SVG with club initials — the system's signature flourish.
components.html: <svg width="48" height="56" viewBox="0 0 56 64" fill="none"><path d="M28 2 L52 14 L52 34 C52 48 41 58 28 62 C15 58 4 48 4 34 L4 14 Z" fill="#3d7bf5" stroke="#2a5fb0" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M28 10 L44 18 L44 33 C44 43 37 50 28 53 C19 50 12 43 12 33 L12 18 Z" fill="#2a5fb0" opacity="0.35"/><text x="28" y="36" text-anchor="middle" fill="#ffffff" font-size="14" font-weight="700" font-family="Inter,sans-serif">FC</text></svg>

components.name: Table Row
components.description: Dense data-table row with zebra striping and a highlighted user row.
components.css: .ds-table { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:12px; color:#e8eaf0; } .ds-table td { padding:8px; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); } .ds-table__team { font-weight:600; } .ds-table__row--alt { background:#191c24; } .ds-table__row--user { background:rgba(61,123,245,0.08); }

narrative.overview:
A tactics-analysis room at 11pm: dark screens, dense data, the glow of a monitor over a desk covered in scouting reports. Nobody designed this to be admired — it was built to be used, for hours, by someone making real decisions about a real squad. The Training Ground rejects the polished sheen of consumer apps and the neon of gaming UI alike. It is closer to a broadcast graphics package or a professional analytics tool than to a mobile game.

The system is near-black by default — #14161c pages, #1b1e26 panels — with a single blue accent (Pitch Blue) doing almost all of the interactive signaling, and three status colors (green/amber/red) carrying every piece of "is this good or bad" information in the data. Separation between regions is done with 1px borders, not shadows; the interface is flat and readable, not a stack of floating cards. Decoration is not the point. The data is the point.
keyCharacteristics: Dark by default — near-black backgrounds, light text, low-chroma panels
One accent color (Pitch Blue), used sparingly for actions and active state
Borders over shadows — regions are separated by 1px lines, not elevation
Status color (green/amber/red) is the only carrier of "good/bad" meaning
Dense tables and stat grids over generous whitespace

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Pitch Blue is the only color used for interactive/action signaling (buttons, active state, links). If a second color starts meaning "click me," the system has failed.

rules.name: The Status-Only Rule
rules.body: Green/amber/red are reserved exclusively for value judgments on data (good/caution/bad). They never appear as decoration, and a given shade always means the same thing everywhere it shows up.

rules.name: The Border-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Regions are separated with a 1px border line first. Reach for elevation only when a border can't do the job — a floating overlay with nothing behind it to border against.

rules.name: The Density Floor Rule
rules.body: Body text sits at 12px, not 14-16px. This is deliberate: the interface is a professional tool viewed on a desktop for long sessions, not a marketing page optimized for skimming.

dos: Do use Pitch Blue for actions and active state only — nowhere else.
Do separate regions with a 1px border first; reach for shadow only for floating overlays.
Do route every status color (fitness, form, risk, position, grade) through utils/statusColors.ts instead of hardcoding hex.
Do reuse PageHeader and TeamCrest for any new screen instead of rebuilding a topbar or a letter-avatar.
Do keep body text at 12px and let layout, not font-size, carry hierarchy.
Do let stat and status colors carry meaning — green = good, amber = caution, red = bad, consistently.
donts: Don't introduce a second accent color for actions/navigation. Pitch Blue is the one accent.
Don't add decorative shadows to flat, in-flow panels — that's enterprise-SaaS card-stacking, and it's explicitly an anti-reference.
Don't use border-left stripes greater than 1px as a colored accent. Use a full border, a background tint, or a badge instead.
Don't use gradient text. If something needs emphasis, use weight or a solid status color.
Don't hardcode a Material-style palette (#4CAF50, #F44336, #2196F3, etc.) for status/position colors — that's the exact drift this system had before utils/statusColors.ts existed.
Don't mix the fms-* and legacy fm-* light-glass component styles into the same screen without reconciling them.
Don't make this feel like a casual game or like enterprise SaaS. It's a serious tool.
